35\u2192\tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n    37\u2192\tv1core \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/core/v1\"\n    38\u2192\tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n    39\u2192\tdiscoverylisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/discovery/v1\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue\"\n    43\u2192\tendpointslicerec \"k8s.io/endpointslice\"\n    44\u2192\tendpointslicemetrics \"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n    46\u2192\tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller\"\n    49\u2192\tendpointslicepkg \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/util/endpointslice\"\n    50\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n    51\u2192)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192const (\n    54\u2192\t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is\n    55\u2192\t// dropped out of the queue. Any sync error, such as a failure to create or\n    56\u2192\t// update an EndpointSlice could trigger a retry. With the current\n    57\u2192\t// rate-limiter in use (1s*2^(numRetries-1)) the following numbers represent\n    58\u2192\t// the sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\n    59\u2192\t//\n    60\u2192\t// 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 64s, 128s, 256s, 512s, 1000s (max)\n    61\u2192\tmaxRetries = 15\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\t// endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay indicates the minimum delay before\n    64\u2192\t// queuing a syncService call after an EndpointSlice changes. If\n    65\u2192\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is greater than this value, it will be used\n    66\u2192\t// instead. This helps batch processing of changes to multiple\n    67\u2192\t// EndpointSlices.\n    68\u2192\tendpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay = 1 * time.Second\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\t// defaultSyncBackOff is the default backoff period for syncService calls.\n    71\u2192\tdefaultSyncBackOff = 1 * time.Second\n    72\u2192\t// maxSyncBackOff is the max backoff period for syncService calls.\n    73\u2192\tmaxSyncBackOff = 1000 * time.Second\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// controllerName is a unique value used with LabelManagedBy to indicated\n    76\u2192\t// the component managing an EndpointSlice.\n    77\u2192\tcontrollerName = \"endpointslice-controller.k8s.io\"\n    78\u2192)\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192// NewController creates and initializes a new Controller\n    81\u2192func NewController(ctx context.Context, podInformer coreinformers.PodInformer,\n    82\u2192\tserviceInformer coreinformers.ServiceInformer,\n    83\u2192\tnodeInformer coreinformers.NodeInformer,\n    84\u2192\tendpointSliceInformer discoveryinformers.EndpointSliceInformer,\n    85\u2192\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32,\n    86\u2192\tclient clientset.Interface,\n    87\u2192\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration,\n    88\u2192) *Controller {\n    89\u2192\tbroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster(record.WithContext(ctx))\n    90\u2192\trecorder := broadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, v1.EventSource{Component: \"endpoint-slice-controller\"})\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\tendpointslicemetrics.RegisterMetrics()\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\tc := &Controller{\n    95\u2192\t\tclient: client,\n    96\u2192\t\t// This is similar to the DefaultControllerRateLimiter, just with a\n    97\u2192\t\t// significantly higher default backoff (1s vs 5ms). This controller\n    98\u2192\t\t// processes events that can require significant EndpointSlice changes,\n    99\u2192\t\t// such as an update to a Service or Deployment. A more significant\n   100\u2192\t\t// rate limit back off here helps ensure that the Controller does not\n   101\u2192\t\t// overwhelm the API Server.\n   102\u2192\t\tqueue: workqueue.NewNamedRateLimitingQueue(workqueue.NewMaxOfRateLimiter(\n   103\u2192\t\t\tworkqueue.NewItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter(defaultSyncBackOff, maxSyncBackOff),\n   104\u2192\t\t\t// 10 qps, 100 bucket size. This is only for retry speed and its\n   105\u2192\t\t\t// only the overall factor (not per item).\n   106\u2192\t\t\t&workqueue.BucketRateLimiter{Limiter: rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(10), 100)},\n   107\u2192\t\t), \"endpoint_slice\"),\n   108\u2192\t\tworkerLoopPeriod: time.Second,\n   109\u2192\t}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192\tserviceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   112\u2192\t\tAddFunc: c.onServiceUpdate,\n   113\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: func(old, cur interface{}) {\n   114\u2192\t\t\tc.onServiceUpdate(cur)\n   115\u2192\t\t},\n   116\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: c.onServiceDelete,\n   117\u2192\t})\n   118\u2192\tc.serviceLister = serviceInformer.Lister()\n   119\u2192\tc.servicesSynced = serviceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\tpodInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   122\u2192\t\tAddFunc:    c.addPod,\n   123\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: c.updatePod,\n   124\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: c.deletePod,\n   125\u2192\t})\n   126\u2192\tc.podLister = podInformer.Lister()\n   127\u2192\tc.podsSynced = podInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192\tc.nodeLister = nodeInformer.Lister()\n   130\u2192\tc.nodesSynced = nodeInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n   133\u2192\tendpointSliceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   134\u2192\t\tAddFunc: c.onEndpointSliceAdd,\n   135\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n   136\u2192\t\t\tc.onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger, oldObj, newObj)\n   137\u2192\t\t},\n   138\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: c.onEndpointSliceDelete,\n   139\u2192\t})\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\tc.endpointSliceLister = endpointSliceInformer.Lister()\n   142\u2192\tc.endpointSlicesSynced = endpointSliceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   143\u2192\tc.endpointSliceTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewEndpointSliceTracker()\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192\tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice = maxEndpointsPerSlice\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192\tc.triggerTimeTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewTriggerTimeTracker()\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\tc.eventBroadcaster = broadcaster\n   150\u2192\tc.eventRecorder = recorder\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\tc.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod = endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.TopologyAwareHints) {\n   155\u2192\t\tnodeInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   156\u2192\t\t\tAddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tc.addNode(logger, obj)\n   158\u2192\t\t\t},\n   159\u2192\t\t\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n   160\u2192\t\t\t\tc.updateNode(logger, oldObj, newObj)\n   161\u2192\t\t\t},\n   162\u2192\t\t\tDeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\n   163\u2192\t\t\t\tc.deleteNode(logger, obj)\n   164\u2192\t\t\t},\n   165\u2192\t\t})\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\t\tc.topologyCache = topologycache.NewTopologyCache()\n   168\u2192\t}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\tc.reconciler = endpointslicerec.NewReconciler(\n   171\u2192\t\tc.client,\n   172\u2192\t\tc.nodeLister,\n   173\u2192\t\tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice,\n   174\u2192\t\tc.endpointSliceTracker,\n   175\u2192\t\tc.topologyCache,\n   176\u2192\t\tc.eventRecorder,\n   177\u2192\t\tcontrollerName,\n   178\u2192\t\tendpointslicerec.WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ServiceTrafficDistribution)),\n   179\u2192\t)\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192\treturn c\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192// Controller manages selector-based service endpoint slices\n   185\u2192type Controller struct {\n   186\u2192\tclient           clientset.Interface\n   187\u2192\teventBroadcaster record.EventBroadcaster\n   188\u2192\teventRecorder    record.EventRecorder\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192\t// serviceLister is able to list/get services and is populated by the\n   191\u2192\t// shared informer passed to NewController\n   192\u2192\tserviceLister corelisters.ServiceLister\n   193\u2192\t// servicesSynced returns true if the service shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   194\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   195\u2192\tservicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192\t// podLister is able to list/get pods and is populated by the\n   198\u2192\t// shared informer passed to NewController\n   199\u2192\tpodLister corelisters.PodLister\n   200\u2192\t// podsSynced returns true if the pod shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   201\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   202\u2192\tpodsSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192\t// endpointSliceLister is able to list/get endpoint slices and is populated by the\n   205\u2192\t// shared informer passed to NewController\n   206\u2192\tendpointSliceLister discoverylisters.EndpointSliceLister\n   207\u2192\t// endpointSlicesSynced returns true if the endpoint slice shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   208\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   209\u2192\tendpointSlicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   210\u2192\t// endpointSliceTracker tracks the list of EndpointSlices and associated\n   211\u2192\t// resource versions expected for each Service. It can help determine if a\n   212\u2192\t// cached EndpointSlice is out of date.\n   213\u2192\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192\t// nodeLister is able to list/get nodes and is populated by the\n   216\u2192\t// shared informer passed to NewController\n   217\u2192\tnodeLister corelisters.NodeLister\n   218\u2192\t// nodesSynced returns true if the node shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   219\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   220\u2192\tnodesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t// reconciler is an util used to reconcile EndpointSlice changes.\n   223\u2192\treconciler *endpointslicerec.Reconciler\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192\t// triggerTimeTracker is an util used to compute and export the\n   226\u2192\t// EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime annotation.\n   227\u2192\ttriggerTimeTracker *endpointsliceutil.TriggerTimeTracker\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192\t// Services that need to be updated. A channel is inappropriate here,\n   230\u2192\t// because it allows services with lots of pods to be serviced much\n   231\u2192\t// more often than services with few pods; it also would cause a\n   232\u2192\t// service that's inserted multiple times to be processed more than\n   233\u2192\t// necessary.\n   234\u2192\tqueue workqueue.RateLimitingInterface\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\t// maxEndpointsPerSlice references the maximum number of endpoints that\n   237\u2192\t// should be added to an EndpointSlice\n   238\u2192\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\t// workerLoopPeriod is the time between worker runs. The workers\n   241\u2192\t// process the queue of service and pod changes\n   242\u2192\tworkerLoopPeriod time.Duration\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is an artificial delay added to all service syncs triggered by pod changes.\n   245\u2192\t// This can be used to reduce overall number of all endpoint slice updates.\n   246\u2192\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n   249\u2192\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n   250\u2192\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n   251\u2192}\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192// Run will not return until stopCh is closed.\n   254\u2192func (c *Controller) Run(ctx context.Context, workers int) {\n   255\u2192\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192\t// Start events processing pipeline.\n   258\u2192\tc.eventBroadcaster.StartLogging(klog.Infof)\n   259\u2192\tc.eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(&v1core.EventSinkImpl{Interface: c.client.CoreV1().Events(\"\")})\n   260\u2192\tdefer c.eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192\tdefer c.queue.ShutDown()\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n   265\u2192\tlogger.Info(\"Starting endpoint slice controller\")\n   266\u2192\tdefer logger.Info(\"Shutting down endpoint slice controller\")\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192\tif !cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(\"endpoint_slice\", ctx.Done(), c.podsSynced, c.servicesSynced, c.endpointSlicesSynced, c.nodesSynced) {\n   269\u2192\t\treturn\n   270\u2192\t}\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Starting worker threads\", \"total\", workers)\n   273\u2192\tfor i := 0; i < workers; i++ {\n   274\u2192\t\tgo wait.Until(func() { c.worker(logger) }, c.workerLoopPeriod, ctx.Done())\n   275\u2192\t}\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192\t<-ctx.Done()\n   278\u2192}\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192// worker runs a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and\n   281\u2192// marks them done. You may run as many of these in parallel as you wish; the\n   282\u2192// workqueue guarantees that they will not end up processing the same service\n   283\u2192// at the same time\n   284\u2192func (c *Controller) worker(logger klog.Logger) {\n   285\u2192\tfor c.processNextWorkItem(logger) {\n   286\u2192\t}\n   287\u2192}\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192func (c *Controller) processNextWorkItem(logger klog.Logger) bool {\n   290\u2192\tcKey, quit := c.queue.Get()\n   291\u2192\tif quit {\n   292\u2192\t\treturn false\n   293\u2192\t}\n   294\u2192\tdefer c.queue.Done(cKey)\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\terr := c.syncService(logger, cKey.(string))\n   297\u2192\tc.handleErr(logger, err, cKey)\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192\treturn true\n   300\u2192}\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192func (c *Controller) handleErr(logger klog.Logger, err error, key interface{}) {\n   303\u2192\ttrackSync(err)\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\tif err == nil {\n   306\u2192\t\tc.queue.Forget(key)\n   307\u2192\t\treturn\n   308\u2192\t}\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192\tif c.queue.NumRequeues(key) < maxRetries {\n   311\u2192\t\tlogger.Info(\"Error syncing endpoint slices for service, retrying\", \"key\", key, \"err\", err)\n   312\u2192\t\tc.queue.AddRateLimited(key)\n   313\u2192\t\treturn\n   314\u2192\t}\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192\tlogger.Info(\"Retry budget exceeded, dropping service out of the queue\", \"key\", key, \"err\", err)\n   317\u2192\tc.queue.Forget(key)\n   318\u2192\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192func (c *Controller) syncService(logger klog.Logger, key string) error {\n   322\u2192\tstartTime := time.Now()\n   323\u2192\tdefer func() {\n   324\u2192\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Finished syncing service endpoint slices\", \"key\", key, \"elapsedTime\", time.Since(startTime))\n   325\u2192\t}()\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192\tnamespace, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)\n   328\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   329\u2192\t\treturn err\n   330\u2192\t}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192\tservice, err := c.serviceLister.Services(namespace).Get(name)\n   333\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   334\u2192\t\tif !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n   335\u2192\t\t\treturn err\n   336\u2192\t\t}\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192\t\tc.triggerTimeTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n   339\u2192\t\tc.reconciler.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n   340\u2192\t\tc.endpointSliceTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n   341\u2192\t\t// The service has been deleted, return nil so that it won't be retried.\n   342\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   343\u2192\t}\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192\tif service.Spec.Type == v1.ServiceTypeExternalName {\n   346\u2192\t\t// services with Type ExternalName receive no endpoints from this controller;\n   347\u2192\t\t// Ref: https://issues.k8s.io/105986\n   348\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   349\u2192\t}\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\n   352\u2192\t\t// services without a selector receive no endpoint slices from this controller;\n   353\u2192\t\t// these services will receive endpoint slices that are created out-of-band via the REST API.\n   354\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   355\u2192\t}\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192\tlogger.V(5).Info(\"About to update endpoint slices for service\", \"key\", key)\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192\tpodLabelSelector := labels.Set(service.Spec.Selector).AsSelectorPreValidated()\n   360\u2192\tpods, err := c.podLister.Pods(service.Namespace).List(podLabelSelector)\n   361\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   362\u2192\t\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\n   363\u2192\t\t// impossible to get this error.\n   364\u2192\t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToListPods\",\n   365\u2192\t\t\t\"Error listing Pods for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   366\u2192\t\treturn err\n   367\u2192\t}\n   368\u2192\n   369\u2192\tesLabelSelector := labels.Set(map[string]string{\n   370\u2192\t\tdiscovery.LabelServiceName: service.Name,\n   371\u2192\t\tdiscovery.LabelManagedBy:   c.reconciler.GetControllerName(),\n   372\u2192\t}).AsSelectorPreValidated()\n   373\u2192\tendpointSlices, err := c.endpointSliceLister.EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).List(esLabelSelector)\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   376\u2192\t\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\n   377\u2192\t\t// impossible to get this error.\n   378\u2192\t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToListEndpointSlices\",\n   379\u2192\t\t\t\"Error listing Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   380\u2192\t\treturn err\n   381\u2192\t}\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192\t// Drop EndpointSlices that have been marked for deletion to prevent the controller from getting stuck.\n   384\u2192\tendpointSlices = dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices)\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192\tif c.endpointSliceTracker.StaleSlices(service, endpointSlices) {\n   387\u2192\t\treturn endpointslicepkg.NewStaleInformerCache(\"EndpointSlice informer cache is out of date\")\n   388\u2192\t}\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\t// We call ComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime here to make sure that the\n   391\u2192\t// state of the trigger time tracker gets updated even if the sync turns out\n   392\u2192\t// to be no-op and we don't update the EndpointSlice objects.\n   393\u2192\tlastChangeTriggerTime := c.triggerTimeTracker.\n   394\u2192\t\tComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime(namespace, service, pods)\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192\terr = c.reconciler.Reconcile(logger, service, pods, endpointSlices, lastChangeTriggerTime)\n   397\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   398\u2192\t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices\",\n   399\u2192\t\t\t\"Error updating Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   400\u2192\t\treturn err\n   401\u2192\t}\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192\treturn nil\n   404\u2192}\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192// onServiceUpdate updates the Service Selector in the cache and queues the Service for processing.\n   407\u2192func (c *Controller) onServiceUpdate(obj interface{}) {\n   408\u2192\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\n   409\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   410\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\", obj, err))\n   411\u2192\t\treturn\n   412\u2192\t}\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192\tc.queue.Add(key)\n   415\u2192}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192// onServiceDelete removes the Service Selector from the cache and queues the Service for processing.\n   418\u2192func (c *Controller) onServiceDelete(obj interface{}) {\n   419\u2192\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\n   420\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   421\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\", obj, err))\n   422\u2192\t\treturn\n   423\u2192\t}\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192\tc.queue.Add(key)\n   426\u2192}\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192// onEndpointSliceAdd queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\n   429\u2192// EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n   430\u2192// endpointSliceTracker.\n   431\u2192func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceAdd(obj interface{}) {\n   432\u2192\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n   433\u2192\tif endpointSlice == nil {\n   434\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceAdd()\"))\n   435\u2192\t\treturn\n   436\u2192\t}\n   437\u2192\tif c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice) {\n   438\u2192\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n   439\u2192\t}\n   440\u2192}\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192// onEndpointSliceUpdate queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if\n   443\u2192// the EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n   444\u2192// endpointSliceTracker or the managed-by value of the EndpointSlice has changed\n   445\u2192// from or to this controller.\n   446\u2192func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger klog.Logger, prevObj, obj interface{}) {\n   447\u2192\tprevEndpointSlice := prevObj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n   448\u2192\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n   449\u2192\tif endpointSlice == nil || prevEndpointSlice == nil {\n   450\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceUpdate()\"))\n   451\u2192\t\treturn\n   452\u2192\t}\n   453\u2192\t// EndpointSlice generation does not change when labels change. Although the\n   454\u2192\t// controller will never change LabelServiceName, users might. This check\n   455\u2192\t// ensures that we handle changes to this label.\n   456\u2192\tsvcName := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n   457\u2192\tprevSvcName := prevEndpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n   458\u2192\tif svcName != prevSvcName {\n   459\u2192\t\tlogger.Info(\"label changed\", \"label\", discovery.LabelServiceName, \"oldService\", prevSvcName, \"newService\", svcName, \"endpointslice\", klog.KObj(endpointSlice))\n   460\u2192\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n   461\u2192\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(prevEndpointSlice)\n   462\u2192\t\treturn\n   463\u2192\t}\n   464\u2192\tif c.reconciler.ManagedByChanged(prevEndpointSlice, endpointSlice) || (c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice)) {\n   465\u2192\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n   466\u2192\t}\n   467\u2192}\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192// onEndpointSliceDelete queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\n   470\u2192// EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n   471\u2192// endpointSliceTracker.\n   472\u2192func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceDelete(obj interface{}) {\n   473\u2192\tendpointSlice := getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj)\n   474\u2192\tif endpointSlice != nil && c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.Has(endpointSlice) {\n   475\u2192\t\t// This returns false if we didn't expect the EndpointSlice to be\n   476\u2192\t\t// deleted. If that is the case, we queue the Service for another sync.\n   477\u2192\t\tif !c.endpointSliceTracker.HandleDeletion(endpointSlice) {\n   478\u2192\t\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n   479\u2192\t\t}\n   480\u2192\t}\n   481\u2192}\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192// queueServiceForEndpointSlice attempts to queue the corresponding Service for\n   484\u2192// the provided EndpointSlice.\n   485\u2192func (c *Controller) queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n   486\u2192\tkey, err := endpointslicerec.ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice)\n   487\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   488\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for EndpointSlice %+v: %v\", endpointSlice, err))\n   489\u2192\t\treturn\n   490\u2192\t}\n   491\u2192\n   492\u2192\t// queue after the max of endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay and\n   493\u2192\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod.\n   494\u2192\tdelay := endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay\n   495\u2192\tif c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod > delay {\n   496\u2192\t\tdelay = c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\n   497\u2192\t}\n   498\u2192\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, delay)\n   499\u2192}\n   500\u2192\n   501\u2192func (c *Controller) addPod(obj interface{}) {\n   502\u2192\tpod := obj.(*v1.Pod)\n   503\u2192\tservices, err := endpointsliceutil.GetPodServiceMemberships(c.serviceLister, pod)\n   504\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   505\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err))\n   506\u2192\t\treturn\n   507\u2192\t}\n   508\u2192\tfor key := range services {\n   509\u2192\t\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\n   510\u2192\t}\n   511\u2192}\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192func (c *Controller) updatePod(old, cur interface{}) {\n   514\u2192\tservices := endpointsliceutil.GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(c.serviceLister, old, cur)\n   515\u2192\tfor key := range services {\n   516\u2192\t\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\n   517\u2192\t}\n   518\u2192}\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192// When a pod is deleted, enqueue the services the pod used to be a member of\n   521\u2192// obj could be an *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item.\n   522\u2192func (c *Controller) deletePod(obj interface{}) {\n   523\u2192\tpod := endpointsliceutil.GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj)\n   524\u2192\tif pod != nil {\n   525\u2192\t\tc.addPod(pod)\n   526\u2192\t}\n   527\u2192}\n   528\u2192\n   529\u2192func (c *Controller) addNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\n   530\u2192\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n   531\u2192}\n   532\u2192\n   533\u2192func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\n   534\u2192\toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\n   535\u2192\tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192\t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\n   538\u2192\t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\n   539\u2192\tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\n   540\u2192\t\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\n   541\u2192\t\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n   542\u2192\t}\n   543\u2192}\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192func (c *Controller) deleteNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\n   546\u2192\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n   547\u2192}\n   548\u2192\n   549\u2192// checkNodeTopologyDistribution updates Nodes in the topology cache and then\n   550\u2192// queues any Services that are past the threshold.\n   551\u2192func (c *Controller) checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger klog.Logger) {\n   552\u2192\tif c.topologyCache == nil {\n   553\u2192\t\treturn\n   554\u2192\t}\n   555\u2192\tnodes, err := c.nodeLister.List(labels.Everything())\n   556\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   557\u2192\t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Error listing Nodes\")\n   558\u2192\t\treturn\n   559\u2192\t}\n   560\u2192\tc.topologyCache.SetNodes(logger, nodes)\n   561\u2192\tserviceKeys := c.topologyCache.GetOverloadedServices()\n   562\u2192\tfor _, serviceKey := range serviceKeys {\n   563\u2192\t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Queuing Service after Node change due to overloading\", \"key\", serviceKey)\n   564\u2192\t\tc.queue.Add(serviceKey)\n   565\u2192\t}\n   566\u2192}\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192// trackSync increments the EndpointSliceSyncs metric with the result of a sync.\n   569\u2192func trackSync(err error) {\n   570\u2192\tmetricLabel := \"success\"\n   571\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   572\u2192\t\tif endpointslicepkg.IsStaleInformerCacheErr(err) {\n   573\u2192\t\t\tmetricLabel = \"stale\"\n   574\u2192\t\t} else {\n   575\u2192\t\t\tmetricLabel = \"error\"\n   576\u2192\t\t}\n   577\u2192\t}\n   578\u2192\tendpointslicemetrics.EndpointSliceSyncs.WithLabelValues(metricLabel).Inc()\n   579\u2192}\n   580\u2192\n   581\u2192func dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\n   582\u2192\tn := 0\n   583\u2192\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n   584\u2192\t\tif endpointSlice.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\n   585\u2192\t\t\tendpointSlices[n] = endpointSlice\n   586\u2192\t\t\tn++\n   587\u2192\t\t}\n   588\u2192\t}\n   589\u2192\treturn endpointSlices[:n]\n   590\u2192}\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192// getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction parses an EndpointSlice from a delete action.\n   593\u2192func getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n   594\u2192\tif endpointSlice, ok := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice); ok {\n   595\u2192\t\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\n   596\u2192\t\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\n   597\u2192\t\treturn endpointSlice\n   598\u2192\t}\n   599\u2192\t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\n   600\u2192\ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\n   601\u2192\tif !ok {\n   602\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\", obj))\n   603\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   604\u2192\t}\n   605\u2192\tendpointSlice, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n   606\u2192\tif !ok {\n   607\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Tombstone contained object that is not a EndpointSlice: %#v\", obj))\n   608\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   609\u2192\t}\n   610\u2192\treturn endpointSlice\n   611\u2192}\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192// isNodeReady returns true if a node is ready; false otherwise.\n   614\u2192func isNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\n   615\u2192\tfor _, c := range node.Status.Conditions {\n   616\u2192\t\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\n   617\u2192\t\t\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n   618\u2192\t\t}\n   619\u2192\t}\n   620\u2192\treturn false\n   621\u2192}\n   622\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
60. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package endpointslice\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    26\u2192\tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    28\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    31\u2192\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    33\u2192\tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n    34\u2192\tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n    39\u2192\tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    41\u2192)\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192// Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n    44\u2192// desired state\n    45\u2192type Reconciler struct {\n    46\u2192\tclient               clientset.Interface\n    47\u2192\tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n    48\u2192\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n    49\u2192\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n    50\u2192\tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n    51\u2192\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n    52\u2192\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n    53\u2192\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n    54\u2192\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\n    55\u2192\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\n    56\u2192\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\n    57\u2192\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\n    58\u2192\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n    59\u2192\tcontrollerName string\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192// WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\n    65\u2192// `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\n    66\u2192func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\n    67\u2192\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\n    68\u2192\t\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\n    69\u2192\t}\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192// endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\n    73\u2192// that logic in Reconciler\n    74\u2192type endpointMeta struct {\n    75\u2192\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\n    76\u2192\taddressType discovery.AddressType\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n    80\u2192// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n    81\u2192// slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n    82\u2192// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n    83\u2192func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n    84\u2192\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n    85\u2192\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n    86\u2192\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\n    89\u2192\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\n    92\u2192\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\n    93\u2192\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\n    94\u2192\t// for further adjustment\n    95\u2192\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n    96\u2192\t\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\n    97\u2192\t\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\n    98\u2192\t\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\", \"existingSlice\", existingSlice, \"err\", err)\n   102\u2192\t\t\t\t} else {\n   103\u2192\t\t\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\n   104\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   105\u2192\t\t\t}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n   108\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   109\u2192\t\t}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192\t\t// add list if it is not on our map\n   112\u2192\t\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\n   113\u2192\t\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\n   114\u2192\t\t}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\n   117\u2192\t}\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// reconcile for existing.\n   120\u2192\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\n   121\u2192\t\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\n   122\u2192\t\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\n   123\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   124\u2192\t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n   125\u2192\t\t}\n   126\u2192\t}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\n   129\u2192\t// by the service\n   130\u2192\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\n   131\u2192\t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n   132\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   133\u2192\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\n   134\u2192\t\t} else {\n   135\u2192\t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\n   136\u2192\t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n   137\u2192\t\t}\n   138\u2192\t}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192// reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n   144\u2192// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n   145\u2192// slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n   146\u2192// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n   147\u2192func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\n   148\u2192\terrs := []error{}\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   151\u2192\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   152\u2192\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   153\u2192\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// Build data structures for existing state.\n   156\u2192\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   157\u2192\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n   158\u2192\t\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\n   159\u2192\t\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\n   160\u2192\t\t\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\n   161\u2192\t\t} else {\n   162\u2192\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n   163\u2192\t\t}\n   164\u2192\t}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192\t// Build data structures for desired state.\n   167\u2192\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\n   168\u2192\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\tfor _, pod := range pods {\n   171\u2192\t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\n   172\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   173\u2192\t\t}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192\t\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\n   176\u2192\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\n   177\u2192\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n   178\u2192\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n   179\u2192\t\t}\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192\t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n   182\u2192\t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n   185\u2192\t\t\t}\n   186\u2192\t\t}\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n   189\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   190\u2192\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n   191\u2192\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n   192\u2192\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n   193\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n   194\u2192\t\t\t}\n   195\u2192\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n   196\u2192\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n   197\u2192\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n   198\u2192\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n   199\u2192\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n   200\u2192\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n   201\u2192\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n   202\u2192\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   206\u2192\t\t\t}\n   207\u2192\t\t}\n   208\u2192\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n   209\u2192\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n   210\u2192\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n   211\u2192\t\t}\n   212\u2192\t}\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\n   215\u2192\ttotalAdded := 0\n   216\u2192\ttotalRemoved := 0\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\n   219\u2192\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\n   220\u2192\t\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\n   221\u2192\t\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\n   222\u2192\t\t\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192\t\ttotalAdded += added\n   225\u2192\t\ttotalRemoved += removed\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192\t\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n   228\u2192\t\t\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\n   229\u2192\t\t\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\n   230\u2192\t\t})\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\n   233\u2192\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\n   234\u2192\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\n   235\u2192\t}\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\n   238\u2192\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\n   239\u2192\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\n   240\u2192\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\n   241\u2192\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\n   242\u2192\t\t}\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\n   246\u2192\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\n   247\u2192\t\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\n   248\u2192\t\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\n   249\u2192\t\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\n   250\u2192\t\t\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.\n   251\u2192\t\t\tslicesToDelete = slicesToDelete[:0]\n   252\u2192\t\t} else {\n   253\u2192\t\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, placeholderSlice)\n   254\u2192\t\t}\n   255\u2192\t\tspMetrics.Set(endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(placeholderSlice.Ports), metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n   256\u2192\t\t\tEndpoints: 0,\n   257\u2192\t\t\tSlices:    1,\n   258\u2192\t\t})\n   259\u2192\t}\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192\tmetrics.EndpointsAddedPerSync.WithLabelValues().Observe(float64(totalAdded))\n   262\u2192\tmetrics.EndpointsRemovedPerSync.WithLabelValues().Observe(float64(totalRemoved))\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\tserviceNN := types.NamespacedName{Name: service.Name, Namespace: service.Namespace}\n   265\u2192\tr.metricsCache.UpdateServicePortCache(serviceNN, spMetrics)\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192\t// Topology hints are assigned per address type. This means it is\n   268\u2192\t// theoretically possible for endpoints of one address type to be assigned\n   269\u2192\t// hints while another endpoints of another address type are not.\n   270\u2192\tsi := &topologycache.SliceInfo{\n   271\u2192\t\tServiceKey:  fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", service.Namespace, service.Name),\n   272\u2192\t\tAddressType: addressType,\n   273\u2192\t\tToCreate:    slicesToCreate,\n   274\u2192\t\tToUpdate:    slicesToUpdate,\n   275\u2192\t\tUnchanged:   unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete),\n   276\u2192\t}\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192\tcanUseTrafficDistribution := r.trafficDistributionEnabled && !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations)\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\t// Check if we need to add/remove hints based on the topology annotation.\n   281\u2192\t//\n   282\u2192\t// This if/else clause can be removed once the annotation has been deprecated.\n   283\u2192\t// Ref: https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-network/4444-service-routing-preference\n   284\u2192\tif r.topologyCache != nil && hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n   285\u2192\t\t// Reaching this point means that we need to configure hints based on the\n   286\u2192\t\t// topology annotation.\n   287\u2192\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, events = r.topologyCache.AddHints(logger, si)\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192\t} else {\n   290\u2192\t\t// Reaching this point means that we will not be configuring hints based on\n   291\u2192\t\t// the topology annotation. We need to do 2 things:\n   292\u2192\t\t//  1. If hints were added previously based on the annotation, we need to\n   293\u2192\t\t//     clear up any locally cached hints from the topologyCache object.\n   294\u2192\t\t//  2. Optionally remove the actual hints from the EndpointSlice if we know\n   295\u2192\t\t//     that the `trafficDistribution` field is also NOT being used. In other\n   296\u2192\t\t//     words, if we know that the `trafficDistribution` field has been\n   297\u2192\t\t//     correctly configured by the customer, we DO NOT remove the hints and\n   298\u2192\t\t//     wait for the trafficDist handlers to correctly configure them. Always\n   299\u2192\t\t//     unconditionally removing hints here (and letting them get readded by\n   300\u2192\t\t//     the trafficDist) adds extra overhead in the form of DeepCopy (done\n   301\u2192\t\t//     within topologyCache.RemoveHints)\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192\t\t// Check 1.\n   304\u2192\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n   305\u2192\t\t\tif r.topologyCache.HasPopulatedHints(si.ServiceKey) {\n   306\u2192\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"TopologyAwareHints annotation has changed, removing hints\", \"serviceKey\", si.ServiceKey, \"addressType\", si.AddressType)\n   307\u2192\t\t\t\tevents = append(events, &topologycache.EventBuilder{\n   308\u2192\t\t\t\t\tEventType: corev1.EventTypeWarning,\n   309\u2192\t\t\t\t\tReason:    \"TopologyAwareHintsDisabled\",\n   310\u2192\t\t\t\t\tMessage:   topologycache.FormatWithAddressType(topologycache.TopologyAwareHintsDisabled, si.AddressType),\n   311\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   312\u2192\t\t\t}\n   313\u2192\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(si.ServiceKey, addressType)\n   314\u2192\t\t}\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192\t\t// Check 2.\n   317\u2192\t\tif !canUseTrafficDistribution {\n   318\u2192\t\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate = topologycache.RemoveHintsFromSlices(si)\n   319\u2192\t\t}\n   320\u2192\t}\n   321\u2192\n   322\u2192\tif canUseTrafficDistribution {\n   323\u2192\t\tr.metricsCache.UpdateTrafficDistributionForService(serviceNN, service.Spec.TrafficDistribution)\n   324\u2192\t\tslicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, _ = trafficdist.ReconcileHints(service.Spec.TrafficDistribution, slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete))\n   325\u2192\t} else {\n   326\u2192\t\tr.metricsCache.UpdateTrafficDistributionForService(serviceNN, nil)\n   327\u2192\t}\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192\terr := r.finalize(service, slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete, triggerTime)\n   330\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   331\u2192\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n   332\u2192\t}\n   333\u2192\tfor _, event := range events {\n   334\u2192\t\tr.eventRecorder.Event(service, event.EventType, event.Reason, event.Message)\n   335\u2192\t}\n   336\u2192\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192}\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192func NewReconciler(client clientset.Interface, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, maxEndpointsPerSlice int32, endpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker, topologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache, eventRecorder record.EventRecorder, controllerName string, options ...ReconcilerOption) *Reconciler {\n   341\u2192\tr := &Reconciler{\n   342\u2192\t\tclient:               client,\n   343\u2192\t\tnodeLister:           nodeLister,\n   344\u2192\t\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice: maxEndpointsPerSlice,\n   345\u2192\t\tendpointSliceTracker: endpointSliceTracker,\n   346\u2192\t\tmetricsCache:         metrics.NewCache(maxEndpointsPerSlice),\n   347\u2192\t\ttopologyCache:        topologyCache,\n   348\u2192\t\teventRecorder:        eventRecorder,\n   349\u2192\t\tcontrollerName:       controllerName,\n   350\u2192\t}\n   351\u2192\tfor _, option := range options {\n   352\u2192\t\toption(r)\n   353\u2192\t}\n   354\u2192\treturn r\n   355\u2192}\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192// placeholderSliceCompare is a conversion func for comparing two placeholder endpoint slices.\n   358\u2192// It only compares the specific fields we care about.\n   359\u2192var placeholderSliceCompare = conversion.EqualitiesOrDie(\n   360\u2192\tfunc(a, b metav1.OwnerReference) bool {\n   361\u2192\t\treturn a.String() == b.String()\n   362\u2192\t},\n   363\u2192\tfunc(a, b metav1.ObjectMeta) bool {\n   364\u2192\t\tif a.Namespace != b.Namespace {\n   365\u2192\t\t\treturn false\n   366\u2192\t\t}\n   367\u2192\t\tfor k, v := range a.Labels {\n   368\u2192\t\t\tif b.Labels[k] != v {\n   369\u2192\t\t\t\treturn false\n   370\u2192\t\t\t}\n   371\u2192\t\t}\n   372\u2192\t\tfor k, v := range b.Labels {\n   373\u2192\t\t\tif a.Labels[k] != v {\n   374\u2192\t\t\t\treturn false\n   375\u2192\t\t\t}\n   376\u2192\t\t}\n   377\u2192\t\treturn true\n   378\u2192\t},\n   379\u2192)\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192// finalize creates, updates, and deletes slices as specified\n   382\u2192func (r *Reconciler) finalize(\n   383\u2192\tservice *corev1.Service,\n   384\u2192\tslicesToCreate,\n   385\u2192\tslicesToUpdate,\n   386\u2192\tslicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\n   387\u2192\ttriggerTime time.Time,\n   388\u2192) error {\n   389\u2192\t// If there are slices to create and delete, change the creates to updates\n   390\u2192\t// of the slices that would otherwise be deleted.\n   391\u2192\tfor i := 0; i < len(slicesToDelete); {\n   392\u2192\t\tif len(slicesToCreate) == 0 {\n   393\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   394\u2192\t\t}\n   395\u2192\t\tsliceToDelete := slicesToDelete[i]\n   396\u2192\t\tslice := slicesToCreate[len(slicesToCreate)-1]\n   397\u2192\t\t// Only update EndpointSlices that are owned by this Service and have\n   398\u2192\t\t// the same AddressType. We need to avoid updating EndpointSlices that\n   399\u2192\t\t// are being garbage collected for an old Service with the same name.\n   400\u2192\t\t// The AddressType field is immutable. Since Services also consider\n   401\u2192\t\t// IPFamily immutable, the only case where this should matter will be\n   402\u2192\t\t// the migration from IP to IPv4 and IPv6 AddressTypes, where there's a\n   403\u2192\t\t// chance EndpointSlices with an IP AddressType would otherwise be\n   404\u2192\t\t// updated to IPv4 or IPv6 without this check.\n   405\u2192\t\tif sliceToDelete.AddressType == slice.AddressType && ownedBy(sliceToDelete, service) {\n   406\u2192\t\t\tslice.Name = sliceToDelete.Name\n   407\u2192\t\t\tslicesToCreate = slicesToCreate[:len(slicesToCreate)-1]\n   408\u2192\t\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slice)\n   409\u2192\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete[:i], slicesToDelete[i+1:]...)\n   410\u2192\t\t} else {\n   411\u2192\t\t\ti++\n   412\u2192\t\t}\n   413\u2192\t}\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192\t// Don't create new EndpointSlices if the Service is pending deletion. This\n   416\u2192\t// is to avoid a potential race condition with the garbage collector where\n   417\u2192\t// it tries to delete EndpointSlices as this controller replaces them.\n   418\u2192\tif service.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\n   419\u2192\t\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToCreate {\n   420\u2192\t\t\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\n   421\u2192\t\t\tcreatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Create(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.CreateOptions{})\n   422\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   423\u2192\t\t\t\t// If the namespace is terminating, creates will continue to fail. Simply drop the item.\n   424\u2192\t\t\t\tif errors.HasStatusCause(err, corev1.NamespaceTerminatingCause) {\n   425\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n   426\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   427\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   428\u2192\t\t\t}\n   429\u2192\t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(createdSlice)\n   430\u2192\t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"create\").Inc()\n   431\u2192\t\t}\n   432\u2192\t}\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToUpdate {\n   435\u2192\t\taddTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice, triggerTime)\n   436\u2192\t\tupdatedSlice, err := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Update(context.TODO(), endpointSlice, metav1.UpdateOptions{})\n   437\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   438\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to update %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   439\u2192\t\t}\n   440\u2192\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.Update(updatedSlice)\n   441\u2192\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"update\").Inc()\n   442\u2192\t}\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192\tfor _, endpointSlice := range slicesToDelete {\n   445\u2192\t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), endpointSlice.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n   446\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   447\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to delete %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %v\", endpointSlice.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n   448\u2192\t\t}\n   449\u2192\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(endpointSlice)\n   450\u2192\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n   451\u2192\t}\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192\ttopologyLabel := \"Disabled\"\n   454\u2192\tif r.topologyCache != nil && hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n   455\u2192\t\ttopologyLabel = \"Auto\"\n   456\u2192\t}\n   457\u2192\tvar trafficDistribution string\n   458\u2192\tif r.trafficDistributionEnabled && !hintsEnabled(service.Annotations) {\n   459\u2192\t\tif service.Spec.TrafficDistribution != nil && *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution == corev1.ServiceTrafficDistributionPreferClose {\n   460\u2192\t\t\ttrafficDistribution = *service.Spec.TrafficDistribution\n   461\u2192\t\t}\n   462\u2192\t}\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192\tnumSlicesChanged := len(slicesToCreate) + len(slicesToUpdate) + len(slicesToDelete)\n   465\u2192\tmetrics.EndpointSlicesChangedPerSync.WithLabelValues(topologyLabel, trafficDistribution).Observe(float64(numSlicesChanged))\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192\treturn nil\n   468\u2192}\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192// reconcileByPortMapping compares the endpoints found in existing slices with\n   471\u2192// the list of desired endpoints and returns lists of slices to create, update,\n   472\u2192// and delete. It also checks that the slices mirror the parent services labels.\n   473\u2192// The logic is split up into several main steps:\n   474\u2192//  1. Iterate through existing slices, delete endpoints that are no longer\n   475\u2192//     desired and update matching endpoints that have changed. It also checks\n   476\u2192//     if the slices have the labels of the parent services, and updates them if not.\n   477\u2192//  2. Iterate through slices that have been modified in 1 and fill them up with\n   478\u2192//     any remaining desired endpoints.\n   479\u2192//  3. If there still desired endpoints left, try to fit them into a previously\n   480\u2192//     unchanged slice and/or create new ones.\n   481\u2192func (r *Reconciler) reconcileByPortMapping(\n   482\u2192\tlogger klog.Logger,\n   483\u2192\tservice *corev1.Service,\n   484\u2192\texistingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice,\n   485\u2192\tdesiredSet endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet,\n   486\u2192\tendpointMeta *endpointMeta,\n   487\u2192) ([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, []*discovery.EndpointSlice, int, int) {\n   488\u2192\tslicesByName := map[string]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   489\u2192\tsliceNamesUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\n   490\u2192\tsliceNamesToUpdate := sets.New[string]()\n   491\u2192\tsliceNamesToDelete := sets.New[string]()\n   492\u2192\tnumRemoved := 0\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192\t// 1. Iterate through existing slices to delete endpoints no longer desired\n   495\u2192\t//    and update endpoints that have changed\n   496\u2192\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n   497\u2192\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = existingSlice\n   498\u2192\t\tnewEndpoints := []discovery.Endpoint{}\n   499\u2192\t\tendpointUpdated := false\n   500\u2192\t\tfor _, endpoint := range existingSlice.Endpoints {\n   501\u2192\t\t\tgot := desiredSet.Get(&endpoint)\n   502\u2192\t\t\t// If endpoint is desired add it to list of endpoints to keep.\n   503\u2192\t\t\tif got != nil {\n   504\u2192\t\t\t\tnewEndpoints = append(newEndpoints, *got)\n   505\u2192\t\t\t\t// If existing version of endpoint doesn't match desired version\n   506\u2192\t\t\t\t// set endpointUpdated to ensure endpoint changes are persisted.\n   507\u2192\t\t\t\tif !endpointsliceutil.EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(got, &endpoint) {\n   508\u2192\t\t\t\t\tendpointUpdated = true\n   509\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   510\u2192\t\t\t\t// once an endpoint has been placed/found in a slice, it no\n   511\u2192\t\t\t\t// longer needs to be handled\n   512\u2192\t\t\t\tdesiredSet.Delete(&endpoint)\n   513\u2192\t\t\t}\n   514\u2192\t\t}\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192\t\t// generate the slice labels and check if parent labels have changed\n   517\u2192\t\tlabels, labelsChanged := setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, existingSlice, service, r.controllerName)\n   518\u2192\n   519\u2192\t\t// If an endpoint was updated or removed, mark for update or delete\n   520\u2192\t\tif endpointUpdated || len(existingSlice.Endpoints) != len(newEndpoints) {\n   521\u2192\t\t\tif len(existingSlice.Endpoints) > len(newEndpoints) {\n   522\u2192\t\t\t\tnumRemoved += len(existingSlice.Endpoints) - len(newEndpoints)\n   523\u2192\t\t\t}\n   524\u2192\t\t\tif len(newEndpoints) == 0 {\n   525\u2192\t\t\t\t// if no endpoints desired in this slice, mark for deletion\n   526\u2192\t\t\t\tsliceNamesToDelete.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\n   527\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   528\u2192\t\t\t\t// otherwise, copy and mark for update\n   529\u2192\t\t\t\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\n   530\u2192\t\t\t\tepSlice.Endpoints = newEndpoints\n   531\u2192\t\t\t\tepSlice.Labels = labels\n   532\u2192\t\t\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\n   533\u2192\t\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\n   534\u2192\t\t\t}\n   535\u2192\t\t} else if labelsChanged {\n   536\u2192\t\t\t// if labels have changed, copy and mark for update\n   537\u2192\t\t\tepSlice := existingSlice.DeepCopy()\n   538\u2192\t\t\tepSlice.Labels = labels\n   539\u2192\t\t\tslicesByName[existingSlice.Name] = epSlice\n   540\u2192\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(epSlice.Name)\n   541\u2192\t\t} else {\n   542\u2192\t\t\t// slices with no changes will be useful if there are leftover endpoints\n   543\u2192\t\t\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Insert(existingSlice.Name)\n   544\u2192\t\t}\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192\tnumAdded := desiredSet.Len()\n   548\u2192\n   549\u2192\t// 2. If we still have desired endpoints to add and slices marked for update,\n   550\u2192\t//    iterate through the slices and fill them up with the desired endpoints.\n   551\u2192\tif desiredSet.Len() > 0 && sliceNamesToUpdate.Len() > 0 {\n   552\u2192\t\tslices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   553\u2192\t\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToUpdate.UnsortedList() {\n   554\u2192\t\t\tslices = append(slices, slicesByName[sliceName])\n   555\u2192\t\t}\n   556\u2192\t\t// Sort endpoint slices by length so we're filling up the fullest ones\n   557\u2192\t\t// first.\n   558\u2192\t\tsort.Sort(endpointSliceEndpointLen(slices))\n   559\u2192\n   560\u2192\t\t// Iterate through slices and fill them up with desired endpoints.\n   561\u2192\t\tfor _, slice := range slices {\n   562\u2192\t\t\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 && len(slice.Endpoints) < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) {\n   563\u2192\t\t\t\tendpoint, _ := desiredSet.PopAny()\n   564\u2192\t\t\t\tslice.Endpoints = append(slice.Endpoints, *endpoint)\n   565\u2192\t\t\t}\n   566\u2192\t\t}\n   567\u2192\t}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192\t// 3. If there are still desired endpoints left at this point, we try to fit\n   570\u2192\t//    the endpoints in a single existing slice. If there are no slices with\n   571\u2192\t//    that capacity, we create new slices for the endpoints.\n   572\u2192\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   573\u2192\n   574\u2192\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 {\n   575\u2192\t\tvar sliceToFill *discovery.EndpointSlice\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192\t\t// If the remaining amounts of endpoints is smaller than the max\n   578\u2192\t\t// endpoints per slice and we have slices that haven't already been\n   579\u2192\t\t// filled, try to fit them in one.\n   580\u2192\t\tif desiredSet.Len() < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) && sliceNamesUnchanged.Len() > 0 {\n   581\u2192\t\t\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   582\u2192\t\t\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesUnchanged.UnsortedList() {\n   583\u2192\t\t\t\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slicesByName[sliceName])\n   584\u2192\t\t\t}\n   585\u2192\t\t\tsliceToFill = getSliceToFill(unchangedSlices, desiredSet.Len(), int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice))\n   586\u2192\t\t}\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192\t\t// If we didn't find a sliceToFill, generate a new empty one.\n   589\u2192\t\tif sliceToFill == nil {\n   590\u2192\t\t\tsliceToFill = newEndpointSlice(logger, service, endpointMeta, r.controllerName)\n   591\u2192\t\t} else {\n   592\u2192\t\t\t// deep copy required to modify this slice.\n   593\u2192\t\t\tsliceToFill = sliceToFill.DeepCopy()\n   594\u2192\t\t\tslicesByName[sliceToFill.Name] = sliceToFill\n   595\u2192\t\t}\n   596\u2192\n   597\u2192\t\t// Fill the slice up with remaining endpoints.\n   598\u2192\t\tfor desiredSet.Len() > 0 && len(sliceToFill.Endpoints) < int(r.maxEndpointsPerSlice) {\n   599\u2192\t\t\tendpoint, _ := desiredSet.PopAny()\n   600\u2192\t\t\tsliceToFill.Endpoints = append(sliceToFill.Endpoints, *endpoint)\n   601\u2192\t\t}\n   602\u2192\n   603\u2192\t\t// New slices will not have a Name set, use this to determine whether\n   604\u2192\t\t// this should be an update or create.\n   605\u2192\t\tif sliceToFill.Name != \"\" {\n   606\u2192\t\t\tsliceNamesToUpdate.Insert(sliceToFill.Name)\n   607\u2192\t\t\tsliceNamesUnchanged.Delete(sliceToFill.Name)\n   608\u2192\t\t} else {\n   609\u2192\t\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, sliceToFill)\n   610\u2192\t\t}\n   611\u2192\t}\n   612\u2192\n   613\u2192\t// Build slicesToUpdate from slice names.\n   614\u2192\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   615\u2192\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToUpdate.UnsortedList() {\n   616\u2192\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, slicesByName[sliceName])\n   617\u2192\t}\n   618\u2192\n   619\u2192\t// Build slicesToDelete from slice names.\n   620\u2192\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   621\u2192\tfor _, sliceName := range sliceNamesToDelete.UnsortedList() {\n   622\u2192\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, slicesByName[sliceName])\n   623\u2192\t}\n   624\u2192\n   625\u2192\treturn slicesToCreate, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete, numAdded, numRemoved\n   626\u2192}\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192func (r *Reconciler) DeleteService(namespace, name string) {\n   629\u2192\tr.metricsCache.DeleteService(types.NamespacedName{Namespace: namespace, Name: name})\n   630\u2192}\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192func (r *Reconciler) GetControllerName() string {\n   633\u2192\treturn r.controllerName\n   634\u2192}\n   635\u2192\n   636\u2192// ManagedByChanged returns true if one of the provided EndpointSlices is\n   637\u2192// managed by the EndpointSlice controller while the other is not.\n   638\u2192func (r *Reconciler) ManagedByChanged(endpointSlice1, endpointSlice2 *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\n   639\u2192\treturn r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice1) != r.ManagedByController(endpointSlice2)\n   640\u2192}\n   641\u2192\n   642\u2192// ManagedByController returns true if the controller of the provided\n   643\u2192// EndpointSlices is the EndpointSlice controller.\n   644\u2192func (r *Reconciler) ManagedByController(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) bool {\n   645\u2192\tmanagedBy := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelManagedBy]\n   646\u2192\treturn managedBy == r.controllerName\n   647\u2192}\n   648\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
61. Grep @ -

TestShouldPodBeInEndpoints(t *testing.T) {\nstaging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/util/controller_utils_test.go-103-\ttestCases := []struct {\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/util/controller_utils_test.go-104-\t\tname               string\n/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/util/controller_utils_test.go-105-\t\tpod
