"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package util\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"crypto/md5\"\n    21\u2192\t\"encoding/hex\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"hash\"\n    24\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n    25\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"github.com/davecgh/go-spew/spew\"\n    28\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    29\u2192\tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    32\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    34\u2192\tv1listers \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    36\u2192)\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192// semanticIgnoreResourceVersion does semantic deep equality checks for objects\n    39\u2192// but excludes ResourceVersion of ObjectReference. They are used when comparing\n    40\u2192// endpoints in Endpoints and EndpointSlice objects to avoid unnecessary updates\n    41\u2192// caused by Pod resourceVersion change.\n    42\u2192var semanticIgnoreResourceVersion = conversion.EqualitiesOrDie(\n    43\u2192\tfunc(a, b v1.ObjectReference) bool {\n    44\u2192\t\ta.ResourceVersion = \"\"\n    45\u2192\t\tb.ResourceVersion = \"\"\n    46\u2192\t\treturn a == b\n    47\u2192\t},\n    48\u2192)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// GetPodServiceMemberships returns a set of Service keys for Services that have\n    51\u2192// a selector matching the given pod.\n    52\u2192func GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, pod *v1.Pod) (sets.String, error) {\n    53\u2192\tset := sets.String{}\n    54\u2192\tservices, err := serviceLister.Services(pod.Namespace).List(labels.Everything())\n    55\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    56\u2192\t\treturn set, err\n    57\u2192\t}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\tfor _, service := range services {\n    60\u2192\t\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\n    61\u2192\t\t\t// if the service has a nil selector this means selectors match nothing, not everything.\n    62\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    63\u2192\t\t}\n    64\u2192\t\tkey, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(service)\n    65\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    66\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, err\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t\tif labels.ValidatedSetSelector(service.Spec.Selector).Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\n    69\u2192\t\t\tset.Insert(key)\n    70\u2192\t\t}\n    71\u2192\t}\n    72\u2192\treturn set, nil\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192// PortMapKey is used to uniquely identify groups of endpoint ports.\n    76\u2192type PortMapKey string\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192// NewPortMapKey generates a PortMapKey from endpoint ports.\n    79\u2192func NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts []discovery.EndpointPort) PortMapKey {\n    80\u2192\tsort.Sort(portsInOrder(endpointPorts))\n    81\u2192\treturn PortMapKey(deepHashObjectToString(endpointPorts))\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// deepHashObjectToString creates a unique hash string from a go object.\n    85\u2192func deepHashObjectToString(objectToWrite interface{}) string {\n    86\u2192\thasher := md5.New()\n    87\u2192\tdeepHashObject(hasher, objectToWrite)\n    88\u2192\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hasher.Sum(nil)[0:])\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192// ShouldPodBeInEndpoints returns true if a specified pod should be in an\n    92\u2192// Endpoints or EndpointSlice resource. Terminating pods are only included if\n    93\u2192// includeTerminating is true.\n    94\u2192func ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod *v1.Pod, includeTerminating bool) bool {\n    95\u2192\t// \"Terminal\" describes when a Pod is complete (in a succeeded or failed phase).\n    96\u2192\t// This is distinct from the \"Terminating\" condition which represents when a Pod\n    97\u2192\t// is being terminated (metadata.deletionTimestamp is non nil).\n    98\u2192\tif isPodTerminal(pod) {\n    99\u2192\t\treturn false\n   100\u2192\t}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192\tif len(pod.Status.PodIP) == 0 && len(pod.Status.PodIPs) == 0 {\n   103\u2192\t\treturn false\n   104\u2192\t}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\tif !includeTerminating && pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n   107\u2192\t\treturn false\n   108\u2192\t}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\treturn true\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192// ShouldSetHostname returns true if the Hostname attribute should be set on an\n   114\u2192// Endpoints Address or EndpointSlice Endpoint.\n   115\u2192func ShouldSetHostname(pod *v1.Pod, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n   116\u2192\treturn len(pod.Spec.Hostname) > 0 && pod.Spec.Subdomain == svc.Name && svc.Namespace == pod.Namespace\n   117\u2192}\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192// podEndpointsChanged returns two boolean values. The first is true if the pod has\n   120\u2192// changed in a way that may change existing endpoints. The second value is true if the\n   121\u2192// pod has changed in a way that may affect which Services it matches.\n   122\u2192func podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod *v1.Pod) (bool, bool) {\n   123\u2192\t// Check if the pod labels have changed, indicating a possible\n   124\u2192\t// change in the service membership\n   125\u2192\tlabelsChanged := false\n   126\u2192\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(newPod.Labels, oldPod.Labels) ||\n   127\u2192\t\t!hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(newPod, oldPod) {\n   128\u2192\t\tlabelsChanged = true\n   129\u2192\t}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// If the pod's deletion timestamp is set, remove endpoint from ready address.\n   132\u2192\tif newPod.DeletionTimestamp != oldPod.DeletionTimestamp {\n   133\u2192\t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n   134\u2192\t}\n   135\u2192\t// If the pod's readiness has changed, the associated endpoint address\n   136\u2192\t// will move from the unready endpoints set to the ready endpoints.\n   137\u2192\t// So for the purposes of an endpoint, a readiness change on a pod\n   138\u2192\t// means we have a changed pod.\n   139\u2192\tif IsPodReady(oldPod) != IsPodReady(newPod) {\n   140\u2192\t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\t// Check if the pod IPs have changed\n   144\u2192\tif len(oldPod.Status.PodIPs) != len(newPod.Status.PodIPs) {\n   145\u2192\t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\tfor i := range oldPod.Status.PodIPs {\n   148\u2192\t\tif oldPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP != newPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP {\n   149\u2192\t\t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n   150\u2192\t\t}\n   151\u2192\t}\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192\t// Endpoints may also reference a pod's Name, Namespace, UID, and NodeName, but\n   154\u2192\t// the first three are immutable, and NodeName is immutable once initially set,\n   155\u2192\t// which happens before the pod gets an IP.\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192\treturn false, labelsChanged\n   158\u2192}\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192// GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange returns a set of Service keys for Services\n   161\u2192// that have potentially been affected by a change to this pod.\n   162\u2192func GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, old, cur interface{}) sets.String {\n   163\u2192\tnewPod := cur.(*v1.Pod)\n   164\u2192\toldPod := old.(*v1.Pod)\n   165\u2192\tif newPod.ResourceVersion == oldPod.ResourceVersion {\n   166\u2192\t\t// Periodic resync will send update events for all known pods.\n   167\u2192\t\t// Two different versions of the same pod will always have different RVs\n   168\u2192\t\treturn sets.String{}\n   169\u2192\t}\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\tpodChanged, labelsChanged := podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod)\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\t// If both the pod and labels are unchanged, no update is needed\n   174\u2192\tif !podChanged && !labelsChanged {\n   175\u2192\t\treturn sets.String{}\n   176\u2192\t}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192\tservices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, newPod)\n   179\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   180\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", newPod.Namespace, newPod.Name, err))\n   181\u2192\t\treturn sets.String{}\n   182\u2192\t}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\tif labelsChanged {\n   185\u2192\t\toldServices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, oldPod)\n   186\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   187\u2192\t\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", oldPod.Namespace, oldPod.Name, err))\n   188\u2192\t\t}\n   189\u2192\t\tservices = determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services, podChanged)\n   190\u2192\t}\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192\treturn services\n   193\u2192}\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192// GetPodFromDeleteAction returns a pointer to a pod if one can be derived from\n   196\u2192// obj (could be a *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item).\n   197\u2192func GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *v1.Pod {\n   198\u2192\tif pod, ok := obj.(*v1.Pod); ok {\n   199\u2192\t\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\n   200\u2192\t\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\n   201\u2192\t\treturn pod\n   202\u2192\t}\n   203\u2192\t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\n   204\u2192\ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\n   205\u2192\tif !ok {\n   206\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\", obj))\n   207\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   208\u2192\t}\n   209\u2192\tpod, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*v1.Pod)\n   210\u2192\tif !ok {\n   211\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"tombstone contained object that is not a Pod: %#v\", obj))\n   212\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   213\u2192\t}\n   214\u2192\treturn pod\n   215\u2192}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192func hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(pod1, pod2 *v1.Pod) bool {\n   218\u2192\treturn pod1.Spec.Hostname == pod2.Spec.Hostname &&\n   219\u2192\t\tpod1.Spec.Subdomain == pod2.Spec.Subdomain\n   220\u2192}\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192func determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services sets.String, podChanged bool) sets.String {\n   223\u2192\tif podChanged {\n   224\u2192\t\t// if the labels and pod changed, all services need to be updated\n   225\u2192\t\tservices = services.Union(oldServices)\n   226\u2192\t} else {\n   227\u2192\t\t// if only the labels changed, services not common to both the new\n   228\u2192\t\t// and old service set (the disjuntive union) need to be updated\n   229\u2192\t\tservices = services.Difference(oldServices).Union(oldServices.Difference(services))\n   230\u2192\t}\n   231\u2192\treturn services\n   232\u2192}\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192// portsInOrder helps sort endpoint ports in a consistent way for hashing.\n   235\u2192type portsInOrder []discovery.EndpointPort\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192func (sl portsInOrder) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n   238\u2192func (sl portsInOrder) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n   239\u2192func (sl portsInOrder) Less(i, j int) bool {\n   240\u2192\th1 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[i])\n   241\u2192\th2 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[j])\n   242\u2192\treturn h1 < h2\n   243\u2192}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192// EndpointsEqualBeyondHash returns true if endpoints have equal attributes\n   246\u2192// but excludes equality checks that would have already been covered with\n   247\u2192// endpoint hashing (see hashEndpoint func for more info) and ignores difference\n   248\u2192// in ResourceVersion of TargetRef.\n   249\u2192func EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(ep1, ep2 *discovery.Endpoint) bool {\n   250\u2192\tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.NodeName, ep2.NodeName) {\n   251\u2192\t\treturn false\n   252\u2192\t}\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192\tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.Zone, ep2.Zone) {\n   255\u2192\t\treturn false\n   256\u2192\t}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Ready, ep2.Conditions.Ready) {\n   259\u2192\t\treturn false\n   260\u2192\t}\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Serving, ep2.Conditions.Serving) {\n   263\u2192\t\treturn false\n   264\u2192\t}\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Terminating, ep2.Conditions.Terminating) {\n   267\u2192\t\treturn false\n   268\u2192\t}\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192\tif !semanticIgnoreResourceVersion.DeepEqual(ep1.TargetRef, ep2.TargetRef) {\n   271\u2192\t\treturn false\n   272\u2192\t}\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\treturn true\n   275\u2192}\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192// boolPtrChanged returns true if a set of bool pointers have different values.\n   278\u2192func boolPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *bool) bool {\n   279\u2192\tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\n   280\u2192\t\treturn true\n   281\u2192\t}\n   282\u2192\tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\n   283\u2192\t\treturn true\n   284\u2192\t}\n   285\u2192\treturn false\n   286\u2192}\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192// stringPtrChanged returns true if a set of string pointers have different values.\n   289\u2192func stringPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *string) bool {\n   290\u2192\tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\n   291\u2192\t\treturn true\n   292\u2192\t}\n   293\u2192\tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\n   294\u2192\t\treturn true\n   295\u2192\t}\n   296\u2192\treturn false\n   297\u2192}\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192// DeepHashObject writes specified object to hash using the spew library\n   300\u2192// which follows pointers and prints actual values of the nested objects\n   301\u2192// ensuring the hash does not change when a pointer changes.\n   302\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/hash\n   303\u2192func deepHashObject(hasher hash.Hash, objectToWrite interface{}) {\n   304\u2192\thasher.Reset()\n   305\u2192\tprinter := spew.ConfigState{\n   306\u2192\t\tIndent:         \" \",\n   307\u2192\t\tSortKeys:       true,\n   308\u2192\t\tDisableMethods: true,\n   309\u2192\t\tSpewKeys:       true,\n   310\u2192\t}\n   311\u2192\tprinter.Fprintf(hasher, \"%#v\", objectToWrite)\n   312\u2192}\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192// IsPodReady returns true if Pods Ready condition is true\n   315\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   316\u2192func IsPodReady(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n   317\u2192\treturn isPodReadyConditionTrue(pod.Status)\n   318\u2192}\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192// IsPodTerminal returns true if a pod is terminal, all containers are stopped and cannot ever regress.\n   321\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   322\u2192func isPodTerminal(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n   323\u2192\treturn isPodPhaseTerminal(pod.Status.Phase)\n   324\u2192}\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192// IsPodPhaseTerminal returns true if the pod's phase is terminal.\n   327\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   328\u2192func isPodPhaseTerminal(phase v1.PodPhase) bool {\n   329\u2192\treturn phase == v1.PodFailed || phase == v1.PodSucceeded\n   330\u2192}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192// IsPodReadyConditionTrue returns true if a pod is ready; false otherwise.\n   333\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   334\u2192func isPodReadyConditionTrue(status v1.PodStatus) bool {\n   335\u2192\tcondition := getPodReadyCondition(&status)\n   336\u2192\treturn condition != nil && condition.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n   337\u2192}\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192// getPodReadyCondition extracts the pod ready condition from the given status and returns that.\n   340\u2192// Returns nil if the condition is not present.\n   341\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   342\u2192func getPodReadyCondition(status *v1.PodStatus) *v1.PodCondition {\n   343\u2192\tif status == nil || status.Conditions == nil {\n   344\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   345\u2192\t}\n   346\u2192\n   347\u2192\tfor i := range status.Conditions {\n   348\u2192\t\tif status.Conditions[i].Type == v1.PodReady {\n   349\u2192\t\t\treturn &status.Conditions[i]\n   350\u2192\t\t}\n   351\u2192\t}\n   352\u2192\treturn nil\n   353\u2192}\n   354\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

  "command": "find /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice -name \"*.go\" -type f | head -20"
}

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package endpointslice\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    21\u2192\t\"time\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    24\u2192\tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n    25\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    26\u2192\tapimachineryvalidation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation\"\n    27\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/intstr\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    31\u2192\tendpointutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    33\u2192\tutilnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n    34\u2192)\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192// podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\n    37\u2192func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n    38\u2192\tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n    39\u2192\tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n    40\u2192\t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n    41\u2192\t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\n    42\u2192\tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\n    43\u2192\tep := discovery.Endpoint{\n    44\u2192\t\tAddresses: getEndpointAddresses(pod.Status, service, addressType),\n    45\u2192\t\tConditions: discovery.EndpointConditions{\n    46\u2192\t\t\tReady:       &ready,\n    47\u2192\t\t\tServing:     &serving,\n    48\u2192\t\t\tTerminating: &terminating,\n    49\u2192\t\t},\n    50\u2192\t\tTargetRef: &v1.ObjectReference{\n    51\u2192\t\t\tKind:      \"Pod\",\n    52\u2192\t\t\tNamespace: pod.ObjectMeta.Namespace,\n    53\u2192\t\t\tName:      pod.ObjectMeta.Name,\n    54\u2192\t\t\tUID:       pod.ObjectMeta.UID,\n    55\u2192\t\t},\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\tif pod.Spec.NodeName != \"\" {\n    59\u2192\t\tep.NodeName = &pod.Spec.NodeName\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\tif node != nil && node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != \"\" {\n    63\u2192\t\tzone := node.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone]\n    64\u2192\t\tep.Zone = &zone\n    65\u2192\t}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tif endpointutil.ShouldSetHostname(pod, service) {\n    68\u2192\t\tep.Hostname = &pod.Spec.Hostname\n    69\u2192\t}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\treturn ep\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192// getEndpointPorts returns a list of EndpointPorts generated from a Service\n    75\u2192// and Pod.\n    76\u2192func getEndpointPorts(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, pod *v1.Pod) []discovery.EndpointPort {\n    77\u2192\tendpointPorts := []discovery.EndpointPort{}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t// Allow headless service not to have ports.\n    80\u2192\tif len(service.Spec.Ports) == 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP == v1.ClusterIPNone {\n    81\u2192\t\treturn endpointPorts\n    82\u2192\t}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\tfor i := range service.Spec.Ports {\n    85\u2192\t\tservicePort := &service.Spec.Ports[i]\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t\tportName := servicePort.Name\n    88\u2192\t\tportProto := servicePort.Protocol\n    89\u2192\t\tportNum, err := findPort(pod, servicePort)\n    90\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    91\u2192\t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Failed to find port for service\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"err\", err)\n    92\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t\ti32PortNum := int32(portNum)\n    96\u2192\t\tendpointPorts = append(endpointPorts, discovery.EndpointPort{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tName:        &portName,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tPort:        &i32PortNum,\n    99\u2192\t\t\tProtocol:    &portProto,\n   100\u2192\t\t\tAppProtocol: servicePort.AppProtocol,\n   101\u2192\t\t})\n   102\u2192\t}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192\treturn endpointPorts\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192// getEndpointAddresses returns a list of addresses generated from a pod status.\n   108\u2192func getEndpointAddresses(podStatus v1.PodStatus, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) []string {\n   109\u2192\taddresses := []string{}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192\tfor _, podIP := range podStatus.PodIPs {\n   112\u2192\t\tisIPv6PodIP := utilnet.IsIPv6String(podIP.IP)\n   113\u2192\t\tif isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv6 {\n   114\u2192\t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n   115\u2192\t\t}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\t\tif !isIPv6PodIP && addressType == discovery.AddressTypeIPv4 {\n   118\u2192\t\t\taddresses = append(addresses, podIP.IP)\n   119\u2192\t\t}\n   120\u2192\t}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\treturn addresses\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192// newEndpointSlice returns an EndpointSlice generated from a service and\n   126\u2192// endpointMeta.\n   127\u2192func newEndpointSlice(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service, endpointMeta *endpointMeta, controllerName string) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n   128\u2192\tgvk := schema.GroupVersionKind{Version: \"v1\", Kind: \"Service\"}\n   129\u2192\townerRef := metav1.NewControllerRef(service, gvk)\n   130\u2192\tepSlice := &discovery.EndpointSlice{\n   131\u2192\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n   132\u2192\t\t\tLabels:          map[string]string{},\n   133\u2192\t\t\tGenerateName:    getEndpointSlicePrefix(service.Name),\n   134\u2192\t\t\tOwnerReferences: []metav1.OwnerReference{*ownerRef},\n   135\u2192\t\t\tNamespace:       service.Namespace,\n   136\u2192\t\t},\n   137\u2192\t\tPorts:       endpointMeta.ports,\n   138\u2192\t\tAddressType: endpointMeta.addressType,\n   139\u2192\t\tEndpoints:   []discovery.Endpoint{},\n   140\u2192\t}\n   141\u2192\t// add parent service labels\n   142\u2192\tepSlice.Labels, _ = setEndpointSliceLabels(logger, epSlice, service, controllerName)\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\treturn epSlice\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192// getEndpointSlicePrefix returns a suitable prefix for an EndpointSlice name.\n   148\u2192func getEndpointSlicePrefix(serviceName string) string {\n   149\u2192\t// use the dash (if the name isn't too long) to make the pod name a bit prettier\n   150\u2192\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s-\", serviceName)\n   151\u2192\tif len(apimachineryvalidation.NameIsDNSSubdomain(prefix, true)) != 0 {\n   152\u2192\t\tprefix = serviceName\n   153\u2192\t}\n   154\u2192\treturn prefix\n   155\u2192}\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192// ownedBy returns true if the provided EndpointSlice is owned by the provided\n   158\u2192// Service.\n   159\u2192func ownedBy(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n   160\u2192\tfor _, o := range endpointSlice.OwnerReferences {\n   161\u2192\t\tif o.UID == svc.UID && o.Kind == \"Service\" && o.APIVersion == \"v1\" {\n   162\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   163\u2192\t\t}\n   164\u2192\t}\n   165\u2192\treturn false\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192// getSliceToFill will return the EndpointSlice that will be closest to full\n   169\u2192// when numEndpoints are added. If no EndpointSlice can be found, a nil pointer\n   170\u2192// will be returned.\n   171\u2192func getSliceToFill(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, numEndpoints, maxEndpoints int) (slice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n   172\u2192\tclosestDiff := maxEndpoints\n   173\u2192\tvar closestSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice\n   174\u2192\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n   175\u2192\t\tcurrentDiff := maxEndpoints - (numEndpoints + len(endpointSlice.Endpoints))\n   176\u2192\t\tif currentDiff >= 0 && currentDiff < closestDiff {\n   177\u2192\t\t\tclosestDiff = currentDiff\n   178\u2192\t\t\tclosestSlice = endpointSlice\n   179\u2192\t\t\tif closestDiff == 0 {\n   180\u2192\t\t\t\treturn closestSlice\n   181\u2192\t\t\t}\n   182\u2192\t\t}\n   183\u2192\t}\n   184\u2192\treturn closestSlice\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192// addTriggerTimeAnnotation adds a triggerTime annotation to an EndpointSlice\n   188\u2192func addTriggerTimeAnnotation(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) {\n   189\u2192\tif endpointSlice.Annotations == nil {\n   190\u2192\t\tendpointSlice.Annotations = make(map[string]string)\n   191\u2192\t}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192\tif !triggerTime.IsZero() {\n   194\u2192\t\tendpointSlice.Annotations[v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime] = triggerTime.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n   195\u2192\t} else { // No new trigger time, clear the annotation.\n   196\u2192\t\tdelete(endpointSlice.Annotations, v1.EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime)\n   197\u2192\t}\n   198\u2192}\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192// ServiceControllerKey returns a controller key for a Service but derived from\n   201\u2192// an EndpointSlice.\n   202\u2192func ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) (string, error) {\n   203\u2192\tif endpointSlice == nil {\n   204\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"nil EndpointSlice passed to ServiceControllerKey()\")\n   205\u2192\t}\n   206\u2192\tserviceName, ok := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n   207\u2192\tif !ok || serviceName == \"\" {\n   208\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"EndpointSlice missing %s label\", discovery.LabelServiceName)\n   209\u2192\t}\n   210\u2192\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", endpointSlice.Namespace, serviceName), nil\n   211\u2192}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192// setEndpointSliceLabels returns a map with the new endpoint slices labels and true if there was an update.\n   214\u2192// Slices labels must be equivalent to the Service labels except for the reserved IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels\n   215\u2192// Changes to IsHeadlessService, LabelServiceName and LabelManagedBy labels on the Service do not result in updates to EndpointSlice labels.\n   216\u2192func setEndpointSliceLabels(logger klog.Logger, epSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice, service *v1.Service, controllerName string) (map[string]string, bool) {\n   217\u2192\tupdated := false\n   218\u2192\tepLabels := make(map[string]string)\n   219\u2192\tsvcLabels := make(map[string]string)\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192\t// check if the endpoint slice and the service have the same labels\n   222\u2192\t// clone current slice labels except the reserved labels\n   223\u2192\tfor key, value := range epSlice.Labels {\n   224\u2192\t\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\n   225\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   226\u2192\t\t}\n   227\u2192\t\t// copy endpoint slice labels\n   228\u2192\t\tepLabels[key] = value\n   229\u2192\t}\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192\tfor key, value := range service.Labels {\n   232\u2192\t\tif isReservedLabelKey(key) {\n   233\u2192\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Service using reserved endpoint slices label\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"skipping\", key, \"label\", value)\n   234\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   235\u2192\t\t}\n   236\u2192\t\t// copy service labels\n   237\u2192\t\tsvcLabels[key] = value\n   238\u2192\t}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\t// if the labels are not identical update the slice with the corresponding service labels\n   241\u2192\tif !apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(epLabels, svcLabels) {\n   242\u2192\t\tupdated = true\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\t// add or remove headless label depending on the service Type\n   246\u2192\tif !isServiceIPSet(service) {\n   247\u2192\t\tsvcLabels[v1.IsHeadlessService] = \"\"\n   248\u2192\t} else {\n   249\u2192\t\tdelete(svcLabels, v1.IsHeadlessService)\n   250\u2192\t}\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192\t// override endpoint slices reserved labels\n   253\u2192\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelServiceName] = service.Name\n   254\u2192\tsvcLabels[discovery.LabelManagedBy] = controllerName\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192\treturn svcLabels, updated\n   257\u2192}\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192// isReservedLabelKey return true if the label is one of the reserved label for slices\n   260\u2192func isReservedLabelKey(label string) bool {\n   261\u2192\tif label == discovery.LabelServiceName ||\n   262\u2192\t\tlabel == discovery.LabelManagedBy ||\n   263\u2192\t\tlabel == v1.IsHeadlessService {\n   264\u2192\t\treturn true\n   265\u2192\t}\n   266\u2192\treturn false\n   267\u2192}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192// endpointSliceEndpointLen helps sort endpoint slices by the number of\n   270\u2192// endpoints they contain.\n   271\u2192type endpointSliceEndpointLen []*discovery.EndpointSlice\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n   274\u2192func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n   275\u2192func (sl endpointSliceEndpointLen) Less(i, j int) bool {\n   276\u2192\treturn len(sl[i].Endpoints) > len(sl[j].Endpoints)\n   277\u2192}\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192// returns a map of address types used by a service\n   280\u2192func getAddressTypesForService(logger klog.Logger, service *v1.Service) sets.Set[discovery.AddressType] {\n   281\u2192\tserviceSupportedAddresses := sets.New[discovery.AddressType]()\n   282\u2192\t// TODO: (khenidak) when address types are removed in favor of\n   283\u2192\t// v1.IPFamily this will need to be removed, and work directly with\n   284\u2192\t// v1.IPFamily types\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192\t// IMPORTANT: we assume that IP of (discovery.AddressType enum) is never in use\n   287\u2192\t// as it gets deprecated\n   288\u2192\tfor _, family := range service.Spec.IPFamilies {\n   289\u2192\t\tif family == v1.IPv4Protocol {\n   290\u2192\t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\n   291\u2192\t\t}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192\t\tif family == v1.IPv6Protocol {\n   294\u2192\t\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n   295\u2192\t\t}\n   296\u2192\t}\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192\tif serviceSupportedAddresses.Len() > 0 {\n   299\u2192\t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses // we have found families for this service\n   300\u2192\t}\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192\t// TODO (khenidak) remove when (1) dual stack becomes\n   303\u2192\t// enabled by default (2) v1.19 falls off supported versions\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\t// Why do we need this:\n   306\u2192\t// a cluster being upgraded to the new apis\n   307\u2192\t// will have service.spec.IPFamilies: nil\n   308\u2192\t// if the controller manager connected to old api\n   309\u2192\t// server. This will have the nasty side effect of\n   310\u2192\t// removing all slices already created for this service.\n   311\u2192\t// this will disable all routing to service vip (ClusterIP)\n   312\u2192\t// this ensures that this does not happen. Same for headless services\n   313\u2192\t// we assume it is dual stack, until they get defaulted by *new* api-server\n   314\u2192\t// this ensures that traffic is not disrupted  until then. But *may*\n   315\u2192\t// include undesired families for headless services until then.\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192\tif len(service.Spec.ClusterIP) > 0 && service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone { // headfull\n   318\u2192\t\taddrType := discovery.AddressTypeIPv4\n   319\u2192\t\tif utilnet.IsIPv6String(service.Spec.ClusterIP) {\n   320\u2192\t\t\taddrType = discovery.AddressTypeIPv6\n   321\u2192\t\t}\n   322\u2192\t\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(addrType)\n   323\u2192\t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for service. This could happen if controller manager is connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. EndpointSlices for this Service will use addressType as the IP Family based on familyOf(ClusterIP).\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"addressType\", addrType, \"clusterIP\", service.Spec.ClusterIP)\n   324\u2192\t\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n   325\u2192\t}\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192\t// headless\n   328\u2192\t// for now we assume two families. This should have minimal side effect\n   329\u2192\t// if the service is headless with no selector, then this will remain the case\n   330\u2192\t// if the service is headless with selector then chances are pods are still using single family\n   331\u2192\t// since kubelet will need to restart in order to start patching pod status with multiple ips\n   332\u2192\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv4)\n   333\u2192\tserviceSupportedAddresses.Insert(discovery.AddressTypeIPv6)\n   334\u2192\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Couldn't find ipfamilies for headless service, likely because controller manager is likely connected to an old apiserver that does not support ip families yet. The service endpoint slice will use dual stack families until api-server default it correctly\", \"service\", klog.KObj(service))\n   335\u2192\treturn serviceSupportedAddresses\n   336\u2192}\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192func unchangedSlices(existingSlices, slicesToUpdate, slicesToDelete []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\n   339\u2192\tchangedSliceNames := sets.New[string]()\n   340\u2192\tfor _, slice := range slicesToUpdate {\n   341\u2192\t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n   342\u2192\t}\n   343\u2192\tfor _, slice := range slicesToDelete {\n   344\u2192\t\tchangedSliceNames.Insert(slice.Name)\n   345\u2192\t}\n   346\u2192\tunchangedSlices := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n   347\u2192\tfor _, slice := range existingSlices {\n   348\u2192\t\tif !changedSliceNames.Has(slice.Name) {\n   349\u2192\t\t\tunchangedSlices = append(unchangedSlices, slice)\n   350\u2192\t\t}\n   351\u2192\t}\n   352\u2192\n   353\u2192\treturn unchangedSlices\n   354\u2192}\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192// hintsEnabled returns true if the provided annotations include either\n   357\u2192// v1.AnnotationTopologyMode or v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints key\n   358\u2192// with a value set to \"Auto\" or \"auto\". When both are set,\n   359\u2192// v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints has precedence.\n   360\u2192func hintsEnabled(annotations map[string]string) bool {\n   361\u2192\tval, ok := annotations[v1.DeprecatedAnnotationTopologyAwareHints]\n   362\u2192\tif !ok {\n   363\u2192\t\tval, ok = annotations[v1.AnnotationTopologyMode]\n   364\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   365\u2192\t\t\treturn false\n   366\u2192\t\t}\n   367\u2192\t}\n   368\u2192\treturn val == \"Auto\" || val == \"auto\"\n   369\u2192}\n   370\u2192\n   371\u2192// isServiceIPSet aims to check if the service's ClusterIP is set or not\n   372\u2192// the objective is not to perform validation here\n   373\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\n   374\u2192func isServiceIPSet(service *v1.Service) bool {\n   375\u2192\treturn service.Spec.ClusterIP != v1.ClusterIPNone && service.Spec.ClusterIP != \"\"\n   376\u2192}\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192// findPort locates the container port for the given pod and portName.  If the\n   379\u2192// targetPort is a number, use that.  If the targetPort is a string, look that\n   380\u2192// string up in all named ports in all containers in the target pod.  If no\n   381\u2192// match is found, fail.\n   382\u2192// copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n   383\u2192func findPort(pod *v1.Pod, svcPort *v1.ServicePort) (int, error) {\n   384\u2192\tportName := svcPort.TargetPort\n   385\u2192\tswitch portName.Type {\n   386\u2192\tcase intstr.String:\n   387\u2192\t\tname := portName.StrVal\n   388\u2192\t\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\n   389\u2192\t\t\tfor _, port := range container.Ports {\n   390\u2192\t\t\t\tif port.Name == name && port.Protocol == svcPort.Protocol {\n   391\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn int(port.ContainerPort), nil\n   392\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   393\u2192\t\t\t}\n   394\u2192\t\t}\n   395\u2192\tcase intstr.Int:\n   396\u2192\t\treturn portName.IntValue(), nil\n   397\u2192\t}\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no suitable port for manifest: %s\", pod.UID)\n   400\u2192}\n   401\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

17\u2192package endpoint\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"math\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    26\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    28\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    31\u2192\tutilruntime 40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue\"\n    42\u2192\tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/endpoints\"\n    45\u2192\tpodutil \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\"\n    46\u2192\tapi \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core\"\n    47\u2192\thelper \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller\"\n    49\u2192\tutillabels \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/labels\"\n    50\u2192\tutilnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n    51\u2192)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192const (\n    54\u2192\t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is dropped out of the queue.\n    55\u2192\t// With the current rate-limiter in use (5ms*2^(maxRetries-1)) the following numbers represent the\n    56\u2192\t// sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\n    57\u2192\t//\n    58\u2192\t// 5ms, 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms, 320ms, 640ms, 1.3s, 2.6s, 5.1s, 10.2s, 20.4s, 41s, 82s\n    59\u2192\tmaxRetries = 15\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t// maxCapacity represents the maximum number of addresses that should be\n    62\u2192\t// stored in an Endpoints resource. In a future release, this controller\n    63\u2192\t// may truncate endpoints exceeding this length.\n    64\u2192\tmaxCapacity = 1000\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\t// truncated is a possible value for `endpoints.kubernetes.io/over-capacity` annotation on an\n    67\u2192\t// endpoint resource and indicates that the number of endpoints have been truncated to\n    68\u2192\t// maxCapacity\n    69\u2192\ttruncated = \"truncated\"\n    70\u2192)\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192// NewEndpointController returns a new *Controller.\n    73\u2192func NewEndpointController(ctx context.Context, podInformer coreinformers.PodInformer, serviceInformer coreinformers.ServiceInformer,\n    74\u2192\tendpointsInformer coreinformers.EndpointsInformer, client clientset.Interface, endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration) *Controller {\n    75\u2192\tbroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster(record.WithContext(ctx))\n    76\u2192\trecorder := broadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, v1.EventSource{Component: \"endpoint-controller\"})\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\te := &Controller{\n    79\u2192\t\tclient:           client,\n    80\u2192\t\tqueue:            workqueue.NewNamedRateLimitingQueue(workqueue.DefaultControllerRateLimiter(), \"endpoint\"),\n    81\u2192\t\tworkerLoopPeriod: time.Second,\n    82\u2192\t}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\tserviceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n    85\u2192\t\tAddFunc: e.onServiceUpdate,\n    86\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: func(old, cur interface{}) {\n    87\u2192\t\t\te.onServiceUpdate(cur)\n    88\u2192\t\t},\n    89\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: e.onServiceDelete,\n    90\u2192\t})\n    91\u2192\te.serviceLister = serviceInformer.Lister()\n    92\u2192\te.servicesSynced = serviceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\tpodInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n    95\u2192\t\tAddFunc:    e.addPod,\n    96\u2192\t\tUpdateFunc: e.updatePod,\n    97\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: e.deletePod,\n    98\u2192\t})\n    99\u2192\te.podLister = podInformer.Lister()\n   100\u2192\te.podsSynced = podInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192\tendpointsInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n   103\u2192\t\tDeleteFunc: e.onEndpointsDelete,\n   104\u2192\t})\n   105\u2192\te.endpointsLister = endpointsInformer.Lister()\n   106\u2192\te.endpointsSynced = endpointsInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\te.triggerTimeTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewTriggerTimeTracker()\n   109\u2192\te.eventBroadcaster = broadcaster\n   110\u2192\te.eventRecorder = recorder\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\te.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod = endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192\treturn e\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// Controller manages selector-based service endpoints.\n   118\u2192type Controller struct {\n   119\u2192\tclient           clientset.Interface\n   120\u2192\teventBroadcaster record.EventBroadcaster\n   121\u2192\teventRecorder    record.EventRecorder\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192\t// serviceLister is able to list/get services and is populated by the shared informer passed to\n   124\u2192\t// NewEndpointController.\n   125\u2192\tserviceLister corelisters.ServiceLister\n   126\u2192\t// servicesSynced returns true if the service shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   127\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   128\u2192\tservicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\t// podLister is able to list/get pods and is populated by the shared informer passed to\n   131\u2192\t// NewEndpointController.\n   132\u2192\tpodLister corelisters.PodLister\n   133\u2192\t// podsSynced returns true if the pod shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   134\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   135\u2192\tpodsSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192\t// endpointsLister is able to list/get endpoints and is populated by the shared informer passed to\n   138\u2192\t// NewEndpointController.\n   139\u2192\tendpointsLister corelisters.EndpointsLister\n   140\u2192\t// endpointsSynced returns true if the endpoints shared informer has been synced at least once.\n   141\u2192\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n   142\u2192\tendpointsSynced cache.InformerSynced\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192\t// Services that need to be updated. A channel is inappropriate here,\n   145\u2192\t// because it allows services with lots of pods to be serviced much\n   146\u2192\t// more often than services with few pods; it also would cause a\n   147\u2192\t// service that's inserted multiple times to be processed more than\n   148\u2192\t// necessary.\n   149\u2192\tqueue workqueue.RateLimitingInterface\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t// workerLoopPeriod is the time between worker runs. The workers process the queue of service and pod changes.\n   152\u2192\tworkerLoopPeriod time.Duration\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192\t// triggerTimeTracker is an util used to compute and export the EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime\n   155\u2192\t// annotation.\n   156\u2192\ttriggerTimeTracker *endpointsliceutil.TriggerTimeTracker\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\n   159\u2192}\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192// Run will not return until stopCh is closed. workers determines how many\n   162\u2192// endpoints will be handled in parallel.\n   163\u2192func (e *Controller) Run(ctx context.Context, workers int) {\n   164\u2192\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192\t// Start events processing pipeline.\n   167\u2192\te.eventBroadcaster.StartStructuredLogging(3)\n   168\u2192\te.eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(&v1core.EventSinkImpl{Interface: e.client.CoreV1().Events(\"\")})\n   169\u2192\tdefer e.eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\tdefer e.queue.ShutDown()\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n   174\u2192\tlogger.Info(\"Starting endpoint controller\")\n   175\u2192\tdefer logger.Info(\"Shutting down endpoint controller\")\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192\tif !cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(\"endpoint\", ctx.Done(), e.podsSynced, e.servicesSynced, e.endpointsSynced) {\n   178\u2192\t\treturn\n   179\u2192\t}\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192\tfor i := 0; i < workers; i++ {\n   182\u2192\t\tgo wait.UntilWithContext(ctx, e.worker, e.workerLoopPeriod)\n   183\u2192\t}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192\tgo func() {\n   186\u2192\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\n   187\u2192\t\te.checkLeftoverEndpoints()\n   188\u2192\t}()\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192\t<-ctx.Done()\n   191\u2192}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192// When a pod is added, figure out what services it will be a member of and\n   194\u2192// enqueue them. obj must have *v1.Pod type.\n   195\u2192func (e *Controller) addPod(obj interface{}) {\n   196\u2192\tpod := obj.(*v1.Pod)\n   197\u2192\tservices, err := endpointsliceutil.GetPodServiceMemberships(e.serviceLister, pod)\n   198\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   199\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err))\n   200\u2192\t\treturn\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

NewReconciler(client clientset.Interface, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, maxEndpointsPerSlice int32, endpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker, topologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache, eventRecorder record.EventRecorder, controllerName string, options ...ReconcilerOption) *Reconciler {\nstaging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go-341-\tr := endpointSliceTracker,\nstaging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go-346-\t\tmetricsCache:         metrics.NewCache(maxEndpointsPerSlice),\nstaging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go-347-\t\ttopologyCache: 78. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go",
  "limit": 250
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\nCopyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage endpointslice\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n\tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n\t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n\tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\n// Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n// desired state\ntype Reconciler struct {\n\tclient               clientset.Interface\n\tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n\tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n\t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\n\t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\n\ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\n\t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\n\teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n\tcontrollerName string\n}\n\ntype ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\n\n// WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\n// `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\nfunc WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\n\treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\n\t\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\n\t}\n}\n\n// endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\n// that logic in Reconciler\ntype endpointMeta struct {\n\tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\n\taddressType discovery.AddressType\n}\n\n// Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n// slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n\n\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\n\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\n\n\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\n\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\n\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\n\t// for further adjustment\n\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n\t\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\n\t\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\n\t\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n\t\t\t\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"Couldn't get key to remove EndpointSlice from topology cache\", \"existingSlice\", existingSlice, \"err\", err)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tr.topologyCache.RemoveHints(svcKey, existingSlice.AddressType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// add list if it is not on our map\n\t\tif _, ok := slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType]; !ok {\n\t\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = make([]*discovery.EndpointSlice, 0, 1)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tslicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType] = append(slicesByAddressType[existingSlice.AddressType], existingSlice)\n\t}\n\n\t// reconcile for existing.\n\tfor addressType := range serviceSupportedAddressesTypes {\n\t\texistingSlices := slicesByAddressType[addressType]\n\t\terr := r.reconcileByAddressType(logger, service, pods, existingSlices, triggerTime, addressType)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// delete those which are of addressType that is no longer supported\n\t// by the service\n\tfor _, sliceToDelete := range slicesToDelete {\n\t\terr := r.client.DiscoveryV1().EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).Delete(context.TODO(), sliceToDelete.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"error deleting %s EndpointSlice for Service %s/%s: %w\", sliceToDelete.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, err))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tr.endpointSliceTracker.ExpectDeletion(sliceToDelete)\n\t\t\tmetrics.EndpointSliceChanges.WithLabelValues(\"delete\").Inc()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errs)\n}\n\n// reconcileByAddressType takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n// compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n// slices (by address type) for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n// to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\nfunc (r *Reconciler) reconcileByAddressType(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time, addressType discovery.AddressType) error {\n\terrs := []error{}\n\n\tslicesToCreate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tslicesToUpdate := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tevents := []*topologycache.EventBuilder{}\n\n\t// Build data structures for existing state.\n\texistingSlicesByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey][]*discovery.EndpointSlice{}\n\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n\t\tif ownedBy(existingSlice, service) {\n\t\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(existingSlice.Ports)\n\t\t\texistingSlicesByPortMap[epHash] = append(existingSlicesByPortMap[epHash], existingSlice)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlice)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build data structures for desired state.\n\tdesiredMetaByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]*endpointMeta{}\n\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap := map[endpointsliceutil.PortMapKey]endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\n\tfor _, pod := range pods {\n\t\tif !endpointsliceutil.ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod, true) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tendpointPorts := getEndpointPorts(logger, service, pod)\n\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n\t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n\t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tspMetrics := metrics.NewServicePortCache()\n\ttotalAdded := 0\n\ttotalRemoved := 0\n\n\t// Determine changes necessary for each group of slices by port map.\n\tfor portMap, desiredEndpoints := range desiredEndpointsByPortMap {\n\t\tnumEndpoints := len(desiredEndpoints)\n\t\tpmSlicesToCreate, pmSlicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToDelete, added, removed := r.reconcileByPortMapping(\n\t\t\tlogger, service, existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap], desiredEndpoints, desiredMetaByPortMap[portMap])\n\n\t\ttotalAdded += added\n\t\ttotalRemoved += removed\n\n\t\tspMetrics.Set(portMap, metrics.EfficiencyInfo{\n\t\t\tEndpoints: numEndpoints,\n\t\t\tSlices:    len(existingSlicesByPortMap[portMap]) + len(pmSlicesToCreate) - len(pmSlicesToDelete),\n\t\t})\n\n\t\tslicesToCreate = append(slicesToCreate, pmSlicesToCreate...)\n\t\tslicesToUpdate = append(slicesToUpdate, pmSlicesToUpdate...)\n\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, pmSlicesToDelete...)\n\t}\n\n\t// If there are unique sets of ports that are no longer desired, mark\n\t// the corresponding endpoint slices for deletion.\n\tfor portMap, existingSlices := range existingSlicesByPortMap {\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[portMap]; !ok {\n\t\t\tslicesToDelete = append(slicesToDelete, existingSlices...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// When no endpoint slices would usually exist, we need to add a placeholder.\n\tif len(existingSlices) == len(slicesToDelete) && len(slicesToCreate) < 1 {\n\t\t// Check for existing placeholder slice outside of the core control flow\n\t\tplaceholderSlice := newEndpointSlice(logger, service, &endpointMeta{ports: []discovery.EndpointPort{}, addressType: addressType}, r.controllerName)\n\t\tif len(slicesToDelete) == 1 && placeholderSliceCompare.DeepEqual(slicesToDelete[0], placeholderSlice) {\n\t\t\t// We are about to unnecessarily delete/recreate the placeholder, remove it now.",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go",
    "numLines": 250,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 648
  },
  "type": "text"
}

pod)\n\t\tepHash := endpointsliceutil.NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts)\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n\t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n\t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip pods on nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic taint, as they should not receive traffic\n\t\tif node != nil && nodeHasNoScheduleNoTrafficTaint(node) {\n\t\t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KObj(node))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\t}",

!ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash] = endpointsliceutil.EndpointSet{}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif _, ok := desiredMetaByPortMap[epHash]; !ok {\n\t\t\tdesiredMetaByPortMap[epHash] = &endpointMeta{\n\t\t\t\taddressType: addressType,\n\t\t\t\tports:       endpointPorts,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\t}",
